evidence: Sam Parr's real roommate-matching app 'Bunk' (podcast calls it 'Bonk') was sold to Apartment List for $15,000 years before this 2019-07 recounting.
The Hustle newsletter, founded by Sam Parr, already reached 1.5M+ subscribers by the 2019 pitch date and was later sold to HubSpot for ~$27M in 2021.
The Hustle's premium research subscription 'Trends' launched publicly in September 2019, shortly after this July 2019 pitch, and grew to 10,000+ paying subscribers before HubSpot sunset it in 2024.
Generic content playbook (blog daily, build list, launch course), not a specific identifiable company.
No specific company found that executed this exact nationwide local-services roll-up pitch as a single traceable venture from this episode.

company: Vista Equity Partners / Thoma Bravo (software roll-up PE model)
evidence: Buying underpriced/undermanaged SaaS companies and raising prices was already an established private-equity playbook (e.g., Vista Equity Partners, Thoma Bravo) well before this 2019 pitch.
Sam Parr and Shaan Puri launched My First Million itself in 2019, the very show this pitch appears on.

company: The Hustle (newsletter + Trends + Hustle Con + podcast)
evidence: By mid-to-late 2019 Sam Parr had assembled exactly this media conglomerate — The Hustle newsletter, the Trends subscription product (launched Sept 2019), Hustle Con events, and the My First Million podcast.
No evidence found of a 'Booya' Q&A app built by Sam Parr or anyone else connected to this pitch.
Product Hunt, the email/community product-discovery platform, was already founded in 2013 by Ryan Hoover, six years before this 2019 pitch.
